subject: Ecology
title: Socotra: The Alien Island
description: A short lesson to help you apply Socotra: The Alien Island.

hook.text: Socotra, off the coast of Yemen, looks like a science fiction landscape—and ecologically, it nearly is.

buildup.text: Dragon blood trees, bottle trees, and desert roses give the island an otherworldly appearance.

discovery.text: A third of Socotra's plant species are found nowhere else, isolated for millions of years.

twist.text: Climate change and overgrazing now threaten species that survived millions of years of isolation.

climax.text: Socotra was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2008 to protect its unique biodiversity.

punchline.text: Earth's most alien landscape is a real island.

quiz.question: What makes Socotra ecologically remarkable?
options: A third of its plants are found nowhere else
It has no plant life at all
It is the largest island on Earth
